textDisplay: THIS MOVIE WAS HORRIBLE!! I came in with high hopes and was really disappointed.  They rushed the 1st fight, and the 2nd was predictable.  They should have used the first 1.5hrs to build up the hate between the fighters and the countries, and then delivered a great fight to end the movie similar to what they did in the first Creed.  He lost that fight and the movie was great because of how they wrote the story.  This filmed seemed rushed, and it will only appeal to women, and those that don't really understand the whole Rocky theme.  In other words is a millennial movie.
textOriginal: THIS MOVIE WAS HORRIBLE!! I came in with high hopes and was really disappointed.  They rushed the 1st fight, and the 2nd was predictable.  They should have used the first 1.5hrs to build up the hate between the fighters and the countries, and then delivered a great fight to end the movie similar to what they did in the first Creed.  He lost that fight and the movie was great because of how they wrote the story.  This filmed seemed rushed, and it will only appeal to women, and those that don't really understand the whole Rocky theme.  In other words is a millennial movie.

textDisplay: Don’t listen to the critics. They compare this movie constantly to the original rocky movies. Consider it a title of its own that has nods to the rocky movies and believe me you will not be let down. I’m not emotional in movies but I almost cried at one point. The movie has some badass scenes and avoids the cliches that break movies for me. Also has a twist ending. Good movie
textOriginal: Don’t listen to the critics. They compare this movie constantly to the original rocky movies. Consider it a title of its own that has nods to the rocky movies and believe me you will not be let down. I’m not emotional in movies but I almost cried at one point. The movie has some badass scenes and avoids the cliches that break movies for me. Also has a twist ending. Good movie

textDisplay:
If you are a big fan of Rocky/Creed film. You owe it to yourself to watch this Masterpiece. 
Creed 2 is a step up to the first one. Besides being louder, bigger among other things. It touches the characters very deep. It fleshes out past storylines and tells a new story bringing back the past and mixing it with the present. 
It makes you feel for both antagonists (Drako and his son) it makes you cry for adonis. It makes you feel so much for Rocky definitely passing the torch. 
This movie was well thought out from beggining to end. 

The score is on point, the many diffent angles the camara shows the fights makes you feel the punches. The training, but most of all THE HEART this movie was made with is just utterly amazing.
textOriginal:
If you are a big fan of Rocky/Creed film. You owe it to yourself to watch this Masterpiece. 
Creed 2 is a step up to the first one. Besides being louder, bigger among other things. It touches the characters very deep. It fleshes out past storylines and tells a new story bringing back the past and mixing it with the present. 
It makes you feel for both antagonists (Drako and his son) it makes you cry for adonis. It makes you feel so much for Rocky definitely passing the torch. 
This movie was well thought out from beggining to end. 

The score is on point, the many diffent angles the camara shows the fights makes you feel the punches. The training, but most of all THE HEART this movie was made with is just utterly amazing.

textDisplay: It was an overall darker and more somber chapter if that's what you mean. After the first 10 minutes, nothing goes good for Adonis. I'm not going to put spoilers, but you get what I mean. Neilson was not pointless at all. She's very important even if she didn't do much. It's a very sore point for Ivan and especially Viktor, who feels abandoned, and during a critical scene, she abandons him again. It makes Ivan and Viktor wonder what they are even fighting for, and Ivan especially discovers what's important to him.
textOriginal: It was an overall darker and more somber chapter if that's what you mean. After the first 10 minutes, nothing goes good for Adonis. Johnny Jimenez I will agree some parts of it were excessively dramatic, but besides Apollo creed, I have more feelings than just ‘resentment’ for the rocky villain. In fact, at one point I was rooting for the antagonist. 

It isn’t your blockbuster masterpiece but it is a well made tribute to the original series.
textOriginal:
Johnny Jimenez I will agree some parts of it were excessively dramatic, but besides Apollo creed, I have more feelings than just ‘resentment’ for the rocky villain. In fact, at one point I was rooting for the antagonist. 

It isn’t your blockbuster masterpiece but it is a well made tribute to the original series.

textOriginal: This movie was A+. Better than Creed 1

textDisplay: It was good but not great. they missed some key beats we needed.( spoiler)  like Rocky and Apollo's widow having a talk about the Drago fight he had 33 years ago that killed her husband.  Some more background about Adonis mother and how Ms. Creed knew about the affair.  Bianca's character becomes too much like Adrian in this one. She had more drive for her career and we needed to see how becoming deaf affected her career.  Donny's character went too dark for this time seemed too self-loathing and self-centered this time around.  He wasn't as likable as the kid we met at Adrian's restaurant with Rocky and he never thanked Rocky properly for everything. Ryan Coogler would have flushed the characters out more in their arcs. And the score was not as amazing as the first movie. Other than that it was a B+ movie. But not as amazing as the first Creed.
textOriginal: It was good but not great. they missed some key beats we needed.( spoiler)  like Rocky and Apollo's widow having a talk about the Drago fight he had 33 years ago that killed her husband.  Some more background about Adonis mother and how Ms. Creed knew about the affair.  Bianca's character becomes too much like Adrian in this one. She had more drive for her career and we needed to see how becoming deaf affected her career.  Donny's character went too dark for this time seemed too self-loathing and self-centered this time around.  He wasn't as likable as the kid we met at Adrian's restaurant with Rocky and he never thanked Rocky properly for everything. Ryan Coogler would have flushed the characters out more in their arcs. And the score was not as amazing as the first movie. Other than that it was a B+ movie. But not as amazing as the first Creed.

textDisplay: It only wrapped production in June 2018...the first trailer wasn't released until a few months ago. While the final fight scene was being filmed in April of this year, the footage was sent to the west coast at the end of every day for post-production (editing) because they were on such a tight schedule to get it finished in order to have the film ready for a November release. So no, there haven't been ads for a year--but maybe you've been hearing about it for awhile because the film was announced several years ago, but delayed by Jordan and Coogler's involvement on Black Panther. Florian Munteanu wasn't cast as Drago's son until January 2018, and Creed II principal photography didn't begin until the end of the first week of March 2018.
textOriginal: It only wrapped production in June 2018...the first trailer wasn't released until a few months ago. While the final fight scene was being filmed in April of this year, the footage was sent to the west coast at the end of every day for post-production (editing) because they were on such a tight schedule to get it finished in order to have the film ready for a November release. So no, there haven't been ads for a year--but maybe you've been hearing about it for awhile because the film was announced several years ago, but delayed by Jordan and Coogler's involvement on Black Panther. Florian Munteanu wasn't cast as Drago's son until January 2018, and Creed II principal photography didn't begin until the end of the first week of March 2018.

textDisplay: Had an opportunity to work with this crew for a couple of days. Sly is very nice and friendly and worked as hard as we did. I totally enjoyed working with the one and only "Rocky"! As to Michael B. Jordan, he apparently is too young and self-absorbed to realize that it's the fans that help him keep a job. He was rude, arrogant and quite the snob! He may be handsome, but looks aren't everything. He wouldn't even acknowledge a hello from many of us working on set. He just stomped off to his trailer and slammed the door after every scene he was in. If not for Sylvester Stallone, other cast members and wanting to hopefully see some of my work on screen, I wouldn't even bother.
textOriginal: Had an opportunity to work with this crew for a couple of days. Sly is very nice and friendly and worked as hard as we did. I totally enjoyed working with the one and only "Rocky"! As to Michael B. Jordan, he apparently is too young and self-absorbed to realize that it's the fans that help him keep a job. He was rude, arrogant and quite the snob! He may be handsome, but looks aren't everything. He wouldn't even acknowledge a hello from many of us working on set. He just stomped off to his trailer and slammed the door after every scene he was in. If not for Sylvester Stallone, other cast members and wanting to hopefully see some of my work on screen, I wouldn't even bother.

textDisplay: Sorry if the truth hurts. We weren't expecting him to be so rude. Maybe it was because we were the ones doing the hard labor and he thought we were beneath him. Hopefully with maturity, he'll learn to have some manners. I saw him in a picture with his mom on TV about a week or so ago and that was not the same person we saw.
textOriginal: Sorry if the truth hurts. We weren't expecting him to be so rude. Maybe it was because we were the ones doing the hard labor and he thought we were beneath him. Hopefully with maturity, he'll learn to have some manners. I saw him in a picture with his mom on TV about a week or so ago and that was not the same person we saw.

textDisplay:
In my mind before the film:
Drago's son is only fighting Adonis to prove to his father, Ivan, that he's not a failure. Something like Adonis went through in the the 1st Creed film.

While, Ivan is still holding so much anger for losing to Rocky, making it personal. Maybe even hate guilt for killing Adonis dad, Apollo.

Yet, Adonis wants revenge and proving that he has what it takes to be the greatest, even if it means fighting his father's killer's son. A personal vendetta.

Lastly, Rocky is still by Adonis no matter any obstacle. Since Adonis reminds Rocky of what it's like still to fight for something, while still developing a father-son relationship. Even in reality its sad that Sylvester's son died, leaving him an empty emotional love-care for a son. In my view, Rocky sees Adonis as his son.

In my mind for the ending of the film:
I only hope Drago's son can be much more than his Father, Ivan. While, Ivan can learn to accept his defeat and possibly make amends to both Rocky and Adonis. While, Adonis becomes the right role model for many fighters, even being a father too. Lastly, Rocky will be thanked and forgiven by Mary-Ann for being the right person needed every time for Adonis, as a father, rather than being a trainer.

Lastly, Rocky is still by Adonis no matter any obstacle. Since Adonis reminds Rocky of what it's like still to fight for something, while still developing a father-son relationship. Even in reality its sad that Sylvester's son died, leaving him an empty emotional love-care for a son. In my view, Rocky sees Adonis as his son.

textDisplay:
I appreciate a brawl but can someone explain how we as US citizens can push the "no bullying" thing while simultaneously promoting the glory of a well taken punch?
Shouldn't it be one or the other?
I think bullies should get kicked in the nuts (repeatedly) and that we should keep making Rock Films.
But if you think bullies should be psychoanalyzed in a safe space, then how can you appreciate a story about physically fighting for your ideals?
textOriginal:

Shouldn't it be one or the other?

But if you think bullies should be psychoanalyzed in a safe space, then how can you appreciate a story about physically fighting for your ideals?

textDisplay: I remember lifting and working out 3 a days 5 times a week. Summer 2k18 i would do a group lift (7 a.m. to 8:30) then walk to the gym from my school about 2-3 miles and work for 2 hours or more then go home and run uphill with bodyweight work. I was going through a lot especially the beginning of that the 2k18 summer so My Emotions shut down and I just trained like a spartan. I wouldn't stop and wont stop till I defeat the devil. I was 15 at the time now im 16. 180lbs squat max 405-410 and bench 250-255 and deadlift 355. This is what reminded me that life and its challenges can evolve you into a different animal. Same beast but another Animal. This is what the trailer reminded me of
textOriginal: I remember lifting and working out 3 a days 5 times a week. Summer 2k18 i would do a group lift (7 a.m. to 8:30) then walk to the gym from my school about 2-3 miles and work for 2 hours or more then go home and run uphill with bodyweight work. I was going through a lot especially the beginning of that the 2k18 summer so My Emotions shut down and I just trained like a spartan. I wouldn't stop and wont stop till I defeat the devil. I was 15 at the time now im 16. 180lbs squat max 405-410 and bench 250-255 and deadlift 355. This is what reminded me that life and its challenges can evolve you into a different animal. Same beast but another Animal. This is what the trailer reminded me of

textDisplay:
Creed so far (i won't say Rocky because i've never watched them) as a franchise is awesome, a thrilling story with background, pain,sacrifices,vengeance, high stakes, wirlwinds of emotions all hidden behind the main premise which is the peak of the movie but what makes the fight so intense and so powerful is not the actual punches and how hard they hit...it's about the background which gives meaning to each punch and second inside the ring...it would just be an average fight like all other fights if not for the whole story.

*mesmerizing*

textDisplay: Two things I hope happen in this movie.  First, I hope Rocky makes things right with Mrs. Creed.  In the first Creed movie, Adonis said Rocky hasn't spoken to her since Apollo's funeral and that's sad.  Second, I would love for Apollo to be able to talk to Adonis, whether it is his voice, a vision, or both, especially considering who Adonis is fighting. Perhaps during the fight, that can happen, especially when Adonis is in trouble and/or he gets knocked down.
textOriginal: Two things I hope happen in this movie.  First, I hope Rocky makes things right with Mrs. Creed.  In the first Creed movie, Adonis said Rocky hasn't spoken to her since Apollo's funeral and that's sad.  Second, I would love for Apollo to be able to talk to Adonis, whether it is his voice, a vision, or both, especially considering who Adonis is fighting. Perhaps during the fight, that can happen, especially when Adonis is in trouble and/or he gets knocked down.

textDisplay: It's the most absurd story line ever. I love it. Kind of hate that its looking to be a grudge match. Drago might have suffered after losing to Rocky, but I feel he would have been athlete enough and fighter enough to know that he lost to a fighter who trained cleanly and at a disadvantage to him. I could think of a bunch of different ways to spin the story.. but over all, I think this one works the best for dramatic reasons. Could be a good movie, by which I mean worth the money I pay to see it.
textOriginal: It's the most absurd story line ever. I love it. Kind of hate that its looking to be a grudge match. Drago might have suffered after losing to Rocky, but I feel he would have been athlete enough and fighter enough to know that he lost to a fighter who trained cleanly and at a disadvantage to him. I could think of a bunch of different ways to spin the story.. but over all, I think this one works the best for dramatic reasons. Could be a good movie, by which I mean worth the money I pay to see it.

textDisplay:
I can see this movie doing well, but they just had to take from of one of the worst and most  illogical of the Rocky movies. Never made sense to me how Apollo Creed went   toe to toe with Rocky Balboa for  two movies, wins by decision in the first Rocky, barely getting beaten in the second after going 15 rounds with Rocky, trains Rocky in the third and get's killed by the Russian Boxer Drago in the second round, THE SECOND ROUND!!!!! Yet, Rocky goes 15 rounds with  Drago TO A WIN. You would have thought that Apollo would have given Drago a better fight than that. He fought Rocky all 15 rounds in the first two movies to almost a stand still and he was that outmatched  with Drago, yet Rocky wasn't???? What??!
    Love you Stallone.....BUT COME ON!!!!

textDisplay: I have really enjoyed the new Rocky movies (6 & 7) and this one looks to be another good one. Rocky 6 seemed to fly under the radar a bit, but his portrayal of an old boxer seeking meaning in his life was great in my mind. The speeches he gave to the city council and to his son were great. Creed 1 did a great job of introducing Adonis and having Rocky be his mentor. Really looking forward to seeing this movie. Based on what happens in it, I could see Sylvester Stallone getting at least an Oscar nomination as a supporting actor. It would come full circle for him and the franchise after Rocky 1 was nominated and won awards.
textOriginal: I have really enjoyed the new Rocky movies (6 & 7) and this one looks to be another good one. Rocky 6 seemed to fly under the radar a bit, but his portrayal of an old boxer seeking meaning in his life was great in my mind. The speeches he gave to the city council and to his son were great. Creed 1 did a great job of introducing Adonis and having Rocky be his mentor. Really looking forward to seeing this movie. Based on what happens in it, I could see Sylvester Stallone getting at least an Oscar nomination as a supporting actor. It would come full circle for him and the franchise after Rocky 1 was nominated and won awards.

textDisplay: I really hope I’m wrong because Rocky is my all time favorite movie character, but after watching this trailer a few times now, I’m starting to think Rocky dies in this movie and is a ghost for Adonis. He is wearing the same clothes throughout almost all of this trailer, they don’t even touch on his health here and he was in pretty bad shape at the end of the last movie, and he is in the ring with Adonis still wearing the same clothes he has on for the rest of the trailer while the other trainers are all wearing Adonis’ colors. Plus Rocky wore Adonis’ colors in the first movie, so why wouldn’t he for this one? Also he doesn’t walk out with Adonis for the fight. I didn’t see Rocky anywhere in the frame for Adonis’ entrance and the press conference scene. I know there is the shot where Rocky and Drago have their staredown at the end of the trailer, but the way the camera cuts away, it’s like Rocky is looking at Drago as a ghost and Drago can feel his presence and is not actually staring back at Rocky. My gut feeling is Rocky dies from his cancer early in this movie and is a ghost for Adonis throughout the rest of the film. I hope I’m wrong, I really do. I love Rocky!
textOriginal: I really hope I’m wrong because Rocky is my all time favorite movie character, but after watching this trailer a few times now, I’m starting to think Rocky dies in this movie and is a ghost for Adonis. He is wearing the same clothes throughout almost all of this trailer, they don’t even touch on his health here and he was in pretty bad shape at the end of the last movie, and he is in the ring with Adonis still wearing the same clothes he has on for the rest of the trailer while the other trainers are all wearing Adonis’ colors. Plus Rocky wore Adonis’ colors in the first movie, so why wouldn’t he for this one? Also he doesn’t walk out with Adonis for the fight. I didn’t see Rocky anywhere in the frame for Adonis’ entrance and the press conference scene. I know there is the shot where Rocky and Drago have their staredown at the end of the trailer, but the way the camera cuts away, it’s like Rocky is looking at Drago as a ghost and Drago can feel his presence and is not actually staring back at Rocky. My gut feeling is Rocky dies from his cancer early in this movie and is a ghost for Adonis throughout the rest of the film. I hope I’m wrong, I really do. I love Rocky!

textDisplay:
I know the standard convention will be that Creed wins and gets revenge on Drago for his father,  if we were to look at this as a movie where we judge Creed. 

But imagine if this is really a Rocky movie instead, and we judge Rocky on whether he would make a different choice this time and save Creed if he must. Imagine if it ends with Creed being pummeled and Rocky saves Creed by throwing in the towel before it is too late. Now THAT would be an interesting way to end the movie.
textOriginal:
I know the standard convention will be that Creed wins and gets revenge on Drago for his father,  if we were to look at this as a movie where we judge Creed. 

But imagine if this is really a Rocky movie instead, and we judge Rocky on whether he would make a different choice this time and save Creed if he must. Imagine if it ends with Creed being pummeled and Rocky saves Creed by throwing in the towel before it is too late. Now THAT would be an interesting way to end the movie.

textDisplay:
I had my hopes up for this but I'm not impressed. Adonis fights Drago's son? That's a really lazy story. A better story would have been if Adonis went to Russia to confront his dad's killer and found a changed man, a broken Drago, post Soviet Union trying to make amends.

 At the end of Rocky IV, the message was "If you can change, and if I can change, we can all change". Whatever happened to that? Drago is still a bad guy? 

A story of redemption would have been far better with Adonis forgiving Drago and training with him to fight another Russian boxer.
textOriginal:
I had my hopes up for this but I'm not impressed. Adonis fights Drago's son? That's a really lazy story. A better story would have been if Adonis went to Russia to confront his dad's killer and found a changed man, a broken Drago, post Soviet Union trying to make amends.

 At the end of Rocky IV, the message was "If you can change, and if I can change, we can all change". Whatever happened to that? Drago is still a bad guy? 

A story of redemption would have been far better with Adonis forgiving Drago and training with him to fight another Russian boxer.

textDisplay: It’s pretty likely he’s gonna win the fight. That’s not the issue. Bigger questions: What will this do to/for his career? How will the fight, and the events leading to it, affect his relationships with his family, including Rocky? What will happen when Rocky and Drago come face to face again after all these years? The Rocky series was about a fighter, a constant underdog, beating the odds, but, with Creed, it’s evolved into a generational story about legacies, about fathers and sons. Adonis is trying to live up to Apollo’s legacy while forging his own. Rocky training Adonis serves as the last chapter of his own legacy. Now we see Viktor Drago trying to redeem his own father’s legacy by destroying the surrogate son of the man who beat his father. And Adonis is now a father himself and he wants to leave a legacy his son can be proud of. Also, Robert Jr. is set to return in this film so we’ll see him dealing with his father confronting the past. This has the makings of the best entry in the Rocky series ever.
textOriginal: It’s pretty likely he’s gonna win the fight. That’s not the issue. Bigger questions: What will this do to/for his career? How will the fight, and the events leading to it, affect his relationships with his family, including Rocky? What will happen when Rocky and Drago come face to face again after all these years? The Rocky series was about a fighter, a constant underdog, beating the odds, but, with Creed, it’s evolved into a generational story about legacies, about fathers and sons. Adonis is trying to live up to Apollo’s legacy while forging his own. Rocky training Adonis serves as the last chapter of his own legacy. Now we see Viktor Drago trying to redeem his own father’s legacy by destroying the surrogate son of the man who beat his father. And Adonis is now a father himself and he wants to leave a legacy his son can be proud of. Also, Robert Jr. is set to return in this film so we’ll see him dealing with his father confronting the past. This has the makings of the best entry in the Rocky series ever.

textDisplay: I never said he wouldn't have.  It was just pandering to the audience IMO; which is the problem I have with it.  If you want to get theoretical though, we could say he wouldn't have won based on the fights we actually saw between him and Rocky during the movies.  The real point here is that it didn't matter and should have been left up to the imagination.  Deciding he lost to Creed didn't help "Creed" the movie in any way.  It was unnecessary appeasement that used the original hero as a stepping stone.
textOriginal: I never said he wouldn't have.  It was just pandering to the audience IMO; which is the problem I have with it.  If you want to get theoretical though, we could say he wouldn't have won based on the fights we actually saw between him and Rocky during the movies.  The real point here is that it didn't matter and should have been left up to the imagination.  Deciding he lost to Creed didn't help "Creed" the movie in any way.  It was unnecessary appeasement that used the original hero as a stepping stone.

textDisplay: Looking forward to this. I do hope that the Dragos are given more characterization to flesh out their story. The "evil russian villain" was good enough for the 1980's Reagan era, but it would feel too generic now. Ivan was a product of his government, not a whole man. I hope they incorporate his own victimization into his motivations.
textOriginal: Looking forward to this. I do hope that the Dragos are given more characterization to flesh out their story. The "evil russian villain" was good enough for the 1980's Reagan era, but it would feel too generic now. Ivan was a product of his government, not a whole man. I hope they incorporate his own victimization into his motivations.

textDisplay: I don't feel like they can make Ivan Drago a bad guy if they follow how Rocky IV ended. Drago seemed respect Rocky as a fighter towards the end of the fight after he stood up to the party saying he fights for himself. They need to maintain that transformation in the character as Drago if they are going to do justice to the best Rocky movie. There is so much more here than a Russia v.s. USA theme and I hope they don't shortchange us with that cheap theme given it's relevance today over good character development.
textOriginal: I don't feel like they can make Ivan Drago a bad guy if they follow how Rocky IV ended. Drago seemed respect Rocky as a fighter towards the end of the fight after he stood up to the party saying he fights for himself. They need to maintain that transformation in the character as Drago if they are going to do justice to the best Rocky movie. There is so much more here than a Russia v.s. USA theme and I hope they don't shortchange us with that cheap theme given it's relevance today over good character development.

textDisplay:
@Shadowiff 

Mediocre motivations are only mediocre once you've broken them down to their core functions instead of how exactly those functions coexist with each other. That's why three-act structures, hero's journey, parallel motivations and other basic story building blocks exist. Just because the guy wins this time, doesn't mean it should take away from the emotion that comes from everything else before that moment comes.
textOriginal:
@Shadowiff

textDisplay:
First thoughts after watching the trailer:
Either history is unfortunately repeated, and Adonis dies in the ring, or Adonis is victorious while sustaining permanent damage that will show itself in future films.
I would prefer the latter if the Creed movies continue in the future, but I'm also not completely in love with Adonis as a character. In that regard, as much as it was emphasized in the first Creed that it was not focusing on Rocky as the main protagonist, I can't help but think of these Creed movies as ultimately Rocky's story. Hence, with this mindset, I would the first scenario where Adonis dies in the ring, perhaps even in Rocky's arms to really emphasize past memories.
All in all, I'm just hoping not to see a perfect happy ending as seeing this trailer giving off a darker vibe. Whether that means Adonis dying, or being victorious in a close death match ultimately leaving him with serious permanent brain damage as Rocky warned him about.
Those were my thoughts I really needed to get off my chest. Tell me what you think.
I also would find it amazing if Adonis beats Drago's son, but dies in his corner after the last round. Different from his father, a peaceful death, but different nonetheless.
textOriginal:
First thoughts after watching the trailer:
Either history is unfortunately repeated, and Adonis dies in the ring, or Adonis is victorious while sustaining permanent damage that will show itself in future films.
I would prefer the latter if the Creed movies continue in the future, but I'm also not completely in love with Adonis as a character. In that regard, as much as it was emphasized in the first Creed that it was not focusing on Rocky as the main protagonist, I can't help but think of these Creed movies as ultimately Rocky's story. Hence, with this mindset, I would the first scenario where Adonis dies in the ring, perhaps even in Rocky's arms to really emphasize past memories.
All in all, I'm just hoping not to see a perfect happy ending as seeing this trailer giving off a darker vibe. Whether that means Adonis dying, or being victorious in a close death match ultimately leaving him with serious permanent brain damage as Rocky warned him about.
Those were my thoughts I really needed to get off my chest. Tell me what you think.
I also would find it amazing if Adonis beats Drago's son, but dies in his corner after the last round. Different from his father, a peaceful death, but different nonetheless.

textDisplay:
Prediction...
1. Adonis now trains out of his dads gym in LA and defeats Wheeler to become champ.
2. Life is good as champion and Adonis starts to get soft.
3. Viktor Drago shows up to the US to challenge Adonis.
4. Adonis asks Rocky to train him for this fight. Rocky tries to talk him out of taking the fight and refuses to train him. 
5. Adonis takes the fight anyway.
6. Viktor KO's Adonis.
7. Adonis become depressed.
8. Rocky comes to his rescue and offers to train him for the rematch with Viktor.
9. They leave the comforts of LA and train in the desert.
10. The rematch with Rocky in his corner is in Russia.
11. Outcome of the fight....we'll find out in November.
textOriginal:
Prediction...
1. Adonis now trains out of his dads gym in LA and defeats Wheeler to become champ.
2. Life is good as champion and Adonis starts to get soft.
3. Viktor Drago shows up to the US to challenge Adonis.
4. Adonis asks Rocky to train him for this fight. Rocky tries to talk him out of taking the fight and refuses to train him. 
5. Adonis takes the fight anyway.
6. Viktor KO's Adonis.
7. Adonis become depressed.
8. Rocky comes to his rescue and offers to train him for the rematch with Viktor.
9. They leave the comforts of LA and train in the desert.
10. The rematch with Rocky in his corner is in Russia.
11. Outcome of the fight....we'll find out in November.

textDisplay:
1st Creed: the upcoming troubled young man with a hard past that's trying to prove who he is rather than his name.
2nd Creed: the match to prove who is the most dominant fighter in his division, while face his father's enemy's son. While being a father to his child, even though he doesn't know how.
3rd Creed: the middle age fighter who is still trying to prove he has what it takes, possibly his last fight that will mark his own name in boxing history, while also be a role model/trainer/father to his children/other kids.
textOriginal:
1st Creed: the upcoming troubled young man with a hard past that's trying to prove who he is rather than his name.
2nd Creed: the match to prove who is the most dominant fighter in his division, while face his father's enemy's son. While being a father to his child, even though he doesn't know how.
3rd Creed: the middle age fighter who is still trying to prove he has what it takes, possibly his last fight that will mark his own name in boxing history, while also be a role model/trainer/father to his children/other kids.

textDisplay: It is the people behind the symbols that make them powerful.  Apollo Creed lost because he did not understand,  not care, and did not want to know about the very deadly fighters was up against.  His fights with Rocky and Ivan were far greater than two fighters.  The flaw beat him again and this time killed him was that it the symbol was not the thing that was punching him in the ring.  Adonis Creed is a fool to take the fight against Ivan's son.  Adonis doesn't have durability that Rocky had and he proved that during the fight with Pretty Ricky.  It will be interesting to see what they do with Creed 2.
textOriginal: It is the people behind the symbols that make them powerful.  Apollo Creed lost because he did not understand,  not care, and did not want to know about the very deadly fighters was up against.  His fights with Rocky and Ivan were far greater than two fighters.  The flaw beat him again and this time killed him was that it the symbol was not the thing that was punching him in the ring.  Adonis Creed is a fool to take the fight against Ivan's son.  Adonis doesn't have durability that Rocky had and he proved that during the fight with Pretty Ricky.  It will be interesting to see what they do with Creed 2.

textDisplay:
1:02 - 1:06 
Rocky says, "That kid was raised in hate", then shows Drago's son running by a vacant in a desolate/rundown area.  He's training in a regular gym (dimly lit) in contrast to the facilities that Drago trained in.  
I'm wondering if this follows the narrative put out in the past of Drago being exiled after his loss to Rocky.
